title: Biology Foundations
description: I would merge topics like this:

title: Cell Biology
description: This is one of the most important biology courses because **everything in biology ultimately happens at the cellular level.**

title: Molecular Biology
description: This course explains **how genetic information is stored, copied, and expressed**, forming the foundation of modern genetics and biotechnology.

title: Genetics
description: This course focuses on **how traits are inherited and how genetic variation shapes life**.

title: Evolution
description: This course explains **how life changes over time and how the diversity of living organisms arose.**

title: Human Physiology
description: This course focuses on **body structure**, while the following course, **Human Physiology**, explains **how each system functions**.

title: Microbiology
description: This course covers the fascinating world of microorganisms and their roles in health, disease, industry, and the environment.

title: Immunology
description: This course explains **how the body defends itself against disease** and is one of the most clinically important areas of biology.

title: Ecology
description: This course explores **how organisms interact with each other and with their environment**, from local ecosystems to the entire biosphere.

title: Biotechnology
description: This course brings together biology, chemistry, and technology to show how living systems are engineered for medicine, agriculture, industry, and environmental applications.

title: Biochemistry
description: This course serves as the bridge between biology, chemistry, medicine, and biotechnology, completing the Biology curriculum.
